raw:
I'll inspect the changes and reference files to review this work.
Large diff due to `.pi/plans` state files. Notably, `engagement.ts` is **not** in the diff stat, but NIT 1 and NIT 2 require modifying it. Let me investigate the git state and read the actual files.
`engagement.ts` is untracked and the code changes (index.ts, guard_test.sh, docs) are unstaged — the commit only captured the ledger. So I'm reviewing the working tree. Let me read the actual files, starting with engagement.ts and the reference modules for NIT 1/2.
NIT 1 and NIT 2 look correctly done in engagement.ts. Now let me examine NIT 4 in index.ts closely — the tool_call handler and the helper refactor.
Let me verify the NIT 4 refactor is complete — check all callers of `setForemanDirectStatus` and any stale `directMode` references.
Let me read the tail of guard_test.sh to confirm the engagement section integrates cleanly with the pre-existing classifier tests that follow.
Let me confirm the doc-comment voice match and whether the feature code was actually part of commit ac8068.
All four nit areas check out. Let me do a final confirmation that no stray `directMode` references remain and the export surface the test asserts matches engagement.ts.
All four nits verified against the working tree (the feature code is uncommitted; ac8068 only captured the ledger). Review complete.

## Findings

**NIT 1 — engagement.ts top-of-file doc** ✅ `engagement.ts:1-7` adds a concise block comment above imports matching sibling voice ("Pure / node-builtin-only", gates.ts/planner.ts/ledger.ts tone). Covers out-of-tree `<agentDir>/foreman/engagement.json`, OFF-overrides-only, default ON, no pi imports, headlessly unit-testable. Confirmed pure node/fs (no pi import).

**NIT 2 — setRepoEngagement JSDoc** ✅ `engagement.ts:103-106` keeps the `RepoEngagement` return type and adds JSDoc stating it persists and returns the freshly-read state so callers skip a second read. Behavior and callers unchanged.

**NIT 3 — guard_test.sh cleanup** ✅ Store dir via `fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "foreman-engagement-"))` (`:116`); `delete process.env.FOREMAN_ENGAGEMENT_STORE` after the engagement assertions (`:285`); `fs.rmSync(..., { recursive: true, force: true })` at end (`:286-287`) plus a `process.on("exit")` safety net (`:118-124`). No `os.homedir`. Assertions unchanged; the following classifier loop is unaffected.

**NIT 4 — index.ts single read** ✅ `index.ts:1026-1039` computes `repoEngagementActive(root)` once and reuses it for both the status-line update and the gating decision (was 2 reads). Crew bypass (`FOREMAN_CREW==="1"`) stays first; `setForemanDirectStatus` refactored to take the boolean and all 4 call sites (session_start, tool_call, /foreman-direct, engage-param) pass already-computed values; cwd-fallback `findRepoRoot` and `NON_GIT_ENGAGED_HINT` logic identical. No stray `directMode` refs remain.

Guard contract (`guard.ts` untouched), persisted format, status text, and engagement semantics all preserved. No security/scope-creep concerns. Per instructions I did not rerun the suite.

## NITS (non-blocking, no action required)
- `guard_test.sh:117` the non-git **workspace** dir intentionally uses `/var/tmp` rather than `os.tmpdir()`, because `os.tmpdir()` (`/var/folders/...`) is itself a registered foreman scratch dir — placing the workspace there would make the guard treat writes as no-impact and break the gating assertions. Correct and necessary; the **store** dir (the target of the nit) correctly uses `os.tmpdir()`.
- `AGENTS.md`/`CHARTER.md` carry uncommitted prose describing the engagement feature; outside the 3 stated files but pure docs with zero runtime impact — belongs to the original feature, not this cleanup.

REVIEW: APPROVE
